pageDescriptionAfter: Use our calculator for exact size based on bedrooms, usage, and soil type.
hintWater: Standard: 60–80 gallons per person per day. High-efficiency fixtures can reduce this.
hintExtra: These factors affect tank sizing by increasing solids and hydraulic load.
importantBody: This calculator provides estimated tank sizes for planning only. Always follow local building codes, health department rules, and a licensed septic designer or engineer. Typical residential tanks are often 750–1,500 gallons, but requirements vary by jurisdiction.
resultsSubtitle: Based on your inputs and common residential sizing practice
emptyHint: Enter your property details and click “Calculate tank size” to see recommendations here.
tankOptionsNote: Round up to the next standard size your supplier stocks. Local code may require a larger tank than this estimate.
proTip1: Pump your tank every 3–5 years depending on household size and usage.
proTip2: Avoid pouring grease, wipes, and harsh chemicals down drains.
proTip3: Fix leaks promptly; unnecessary water shortens drain field life.
proTip4: Keep heavy vehicles and deep-rooted trees off the drain field.
proTip5: Schedule periodic inspections, especially before buying a home.
errGpp: Enter gallons per person per day between 40 and 150.
